annotations.label: Riesz representation theorem connection

anchor.section: (Lead)

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the Frechet-Riesz isometric equivalence between a Hilbert space and its strong dual.

annotations.label: Complex and real Hilbert spaces

anchor.section: Preliminaries and notation
anchor.snippet: is called a complex Hilbert space

annotations.note: The `HilbertSpace` structure covers complete real and complex inner product spaces over `RCLike` scalar fields.

annotations.label: Complexification of real Hilbert space

anchor.section: Preliminaries and notation
anchor.snippet: Every real Hilbert space can be extended

annotations.note: Mathlib verifies the reverse scalar-restriction construction from complex or `RCLike` spaces to real inner product spaces, but I did not find an arbitrary real Hilbert space complexification theorem.

annotations.label: Antilinear map

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: Antilinear maps are represented as semilinear `LinearMap`s using the star ring endomorphism and the `→ₗ⋆` notation.

annotations.label: Linear map

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: `LinearMap` is Mathlib's bundled definition of an additive scalar-preserving map.

annotations.label: Constant maps are linear and antilinear

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: Mathlib has the zero linear and semilinear maps, but arbitrary nonzero constant maps are not linear between vector spaces in the usual bundled sense.

annotations.label: Real linear and antilinear maps coincide

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps
anchor.snippet: the definitions of linear maps and antilinear maps are completely identical

annotations.note: The star-linear infrastructure collapses to ordinary linear behavior for trivial star scalars, but I did not find a named dual-space equality theorem for the real case.

annotations.label: Bounded iff continuous linear map

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps
anchor.snippet: is continuous if and only if it is bounded

annotations.note: This theorem states the equivalence between a linear map being continuous and being a bounded linear map.

annotations.label: Bounded iff continuous antilinear map

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps
anchor.snippet: the same is true of antilinear maps

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the continuous-to-bounded direction for semilinear maps and has constructors from bounds, but I did not find a single antilinear iff declaration.

annotations.label: Inverse of antilinear or linear bijection

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: Bundled continuous linear and semilinear equivalences in Mathlib include inverse maps via `symm`.

annotations.label: Composition of two antilinear maps

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps
anchor.snippet: The composition of two anti linear maps is a linear map

annotations.note: Composition of semilinear maps is formalized generically, specializing to the composition of two conjugate-linear maps being linear.

annotations.label: Functional

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: Mathlib defines linear functionals as the algebraic dual `M →ₗ[R] R`, while the article's generic functional terminology is broader.

annotations.label: Continuous dual space

annotations.note: `StrongDual 𝕜 E` is the Mathlib abbreviation for the continuous dual `E →L[𝕜] 𝕜`.

annotations.label: Continuous anti-dual space

annotations.note: Continuous antilinear functionals are represented by semilinear continuous maps using the `→L⋆` notation, though not by a separate anti-dual abbreviation.

annotations.label: Real dual equals anti-dual

anchor.section: Linear and antilinear maps

annotations.note: The trivial-star continuous star-linear equivalence gives related infrastructure, but I did not find a named theorem identifying real dual and anti-dual spaces.

anchor.snippet: these two notations will be related by the equality

annotations.label: Inner product linear coordinate

anchor.snippet: is linear in its first coordinate

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the opposite convention, proving linearity in the second coordinate and conjugate-linearity in the first.

annotations.label: Fixed second coordinate gives continuous linear functional

anchor.section: Mathematics vs. physics notations and definitions of inner product

annotations.note: Mathlib's `innerSL` gives a continuous linear functional after fixing the first coordinate under its convention, not the article's stated coordinate.

annotations.label: Inner product antilinear coordinate

anchor.snippet: is antilinear in its second coordinate

annotations.note: Mathlib proves conjugate-linearity in the first coordinate, reflecting its convention opposite to the article's coordinate statement.

annotations.label: Fixed first coordinate gives continuous antilinear functional

anchor.section: Mathematics vs. physics notations and definitions of inner product

annotations.note: `innerSLFlip` gives a continuous conjugate-linear functional from the fixed-coordinate inner product, but with Mathlib's coordinate convention rather than the article's.
